expectations.text: Flags the 'verified in integration tests' claim as false (grep confirms zero matches)
expectations.evidence: the message claims "verified in integration tests", but the user has already run grep ... and found zero matches. The claim is a lie.

expectations.text: Identifies all three skipped tests as empty shells with no arrange/act/assert
expectations.evidence: Rule 1 (No empty test shells) cited for all three: 48-hour test ('body is comments only. No arrange/act/assert'), ToBlockedUser ('empty body'), Concurrent ('empty body').

expectations.text: Flags 'TODO: fix later' as a forbidden skip pattern (no tracking link or exit criterion)
expectations.evidence: "TODO: fix later" is explicitly listed in the HYGIENE forbidden-patterns table ("No tracking, no exit criterion"). This is a textbook violation.

expectations.text: Flags 'flaky' skip as forbidden (no tracking link, masks a real issue)
expectations.evidence: "flaky" with no tracking link is explicitly forbidden ("No SLA, no owner, no plan").

expectations.text: Flags Service_CanBeConstructed as a forbidden false-coverage-inflation test
expectations.evidence: Rule 7 (No false coverage inflation) — explicitly lists "Tests that only assert the constructor didn't throw" as forbidden.

expectations.text: Recommends tier escalation (unit -> integration with Docker) for the 48-hour DateTime test instead of skip
expectations.evidence: Delete this test from the unit project and write it in Buckit.Services.IntegrationTests against a real SQL Server (Docker/Testcontainers)

expectations.text: Recommends a Known Gaps / TESTING.md ledger entry for any skip that survives
